title: cleome micrantha
intro: Cleome micrantha, also known as Spider Flower, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its small white or pink flowers that resemble a spider's web. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cleome micrantha include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Cleome micrantha include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
